steps.title: Identify two clear points on the line of best fit
steps.content:
Reading the graph, the line passes through:
  • The y-intercept at $(0, 14)$
  • A point at approximately $(12, 0)$
These are easy to read directly from the grid.

steps.title: Calculate the slope
steps.content:
Using $(x_1, y_1) = (0, 14)$ and $(x_2, y_2) = (12, 0)$:
$$\text{slope} = \frac{0 - 14}{12 - 0} = \frac{-14}{12} \approx -1.17$$
This is closest to $-1.1$ among the four choices.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$-3.3$': Would require a much steeper descent — the line would drop 3.3 units per unit of $x$, far steeper than the graph shows. ✗
  • Choice B — '$-1.1$': Matches the computed slope of $\approx -1.17$. ✓
  • Choice C — '$1.1$': Positive — the line clearly slopes downward, so this is wrong in sign. ✗
  • Choice D — '$3.3$': Positive and steep — both incorrect. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.

explanation:
• Choice A — '$-3.3$': Too steep; the line would drop 3.3 units per 1-unit increase in $x$, inconsistent with the moderate slope shown. ✗
  • Choice B — '$-1.1$': Reading two grid points gives slope $\frac{0-14}{12-0} \approx -1.17$, closest to $-1.1$. ✓
  • Choice C — '$1.1$': The line clearly slopes downward (negative), not upward. ✗
  • Choice D — '$3.3$': Positive and far too large in magnitude. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
